question.text: Under SEBI ICDR Regulations, what is the minimum net tangible asset requirement for an issuer seeking eligibility for an IPO under Regulation 6(1)?

answer.text: Rs. 1 crore in each of the 3 preceding full years
Rs. 5 crore in each of the 3 preceding full years
Rs. 3 crore in each of the 3 preceding full years
Rs. 10 crore in each of the 3 preceding full years

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 6(1) of SEBI ICDR Regulations requires net tangible assets of at least Rs. 3 crore in each of the three preceding full years, with not more than 50% held in monetary assets (unless utilized or committed). **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore is the minimum net worth requirement, not the net tangible assets requirement. Candidates often confuse these two distinct thresholds. **Cross-Exam Context:** This eligibility criterion is also tested in NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) when discussing IPO allotment and in NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) for understanding listing norms. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify all three financial parameters — net tangible assets (Rs. 3 crore), operating profit (Rs. 15 crore average), and net worth (Rs. 1 crore) — when assessing IPO readiness of a client company.
